jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams] - Whee! - Whoo-hoo! - Yeah! - [Groans] D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's signature exclamation during opening credits
jokes.punchline: The contrast between Homer's pain and the kids' joy
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase with family dynamic - iconic but routine for the series

jokes.text: Son of a diddly!
jokes.punchline: His characteristic sanitized swearing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders character moment - his wholesome profanity substitute is both funny and character-defining

jokes.text: You know I like my breakfast fried or chicken-fried.
jokes.punchline: His specific preference for two types of unhealthy preparation
jokes.explanation: Solid Homer food humor that reinforces his unhealthy eating habits

jokes.text: It's a healthy cereal from Europe. Müeslix! [All Shuddering]
jokes.punchline: The entire family's disgusted reaction to foreign health food
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of family unity through disgust at healthy food

jokes.text: They also make Juice-lix.
jokes.setup: Following up on the Müeslix mention
jokes.punchline: Made-up product name that sounds equally unappetizing
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that extends the health food gag

jokes.text: That's Milhouse. And it sounds like he has big news!
jokes.punchline: Bart's ability to diagnose Milhouse's excitement level from doorbell style
jokes.explanation: Smart character comedy showing Bart's familiarity with his friend's patterns

jokes.text: Meet Linguo, the grammar robot. I built him all by myself.
jokes.punchline: A robot specifically designed for grammar correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa project - intellectually pretentious and practical, setting up the episode's tech theme

jokes.text: Me love beer. / I love beer. / Oh, he loves beer. Here, little fella.
jokes.setup: Testing the grammar robot with intentionally bad grammar
jokes.punchline: Homer misinterprets the correction as the robot wanting beer
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding that's perfectly in character - he projects his own desires onto others

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I thought he was a party robot.
jokes.setup: Homer giving beer to the grammar robot
jokes.punchline: His sincere apology based on robot categorization
jokes.explanation: Homer's innocent logic about robot types is endearingly stupid

jokes.text: Oh! This is why I can't have nice things.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's mature exasperation using an adult phrase
jokes.explanation: Lisa's world-weary response to family chaos is relatable and well-delivered

jokes.text: Homer wants a brownie. I'm gonna get one. / Coming in from the left. / Or is it the right? Look out for the reach-around!
jokes.punchline: His sports commentary on his own sneaking attempt
jokes.explanation: Homer treating brownie theft like a sport adds absurd intensity to mundane action

jokes.text: He shoots, he sc- [Screams] My thumb!
jokes.punchline: His triumphant moment interrupted by severe injury
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing cutting off Homer's celebration with disaster

jokes.text: Sorry doesn't put thumbs on the hand, Marge!
jokes.setup: Marge apologizing for cutting off his thumb
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal and dramatic response to her apology
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatically literal complaint is both absurd and strangely logical

jokes.text: Reattach a thumb? This isn't Gattaca!
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre reference to a sci-fi movie about genetic engineering
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific pop culture reference that makes no sense in context, which is what makes it funny

jokes.text: 9-1-1. This better be good.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's unprofessional and dismissive greeting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - treating emergency calls like entertainment

jokes.text: Attempted murder? You'll burn for this- burn in jail!
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's dramatic overreaction and awkward clarification
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's dramatic delivery and self-correction shows his theatrical incompetence

jokes.text: 123 Fake Street. Got it.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum accepting it completely seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum gullibility - the obvious fakeness makes his acceptance funnier

jokes.text: And Harry Potter and all his wizard friends went straight to hell for practicing witchcraft.
jokes.punchline: His religious reinterpretation of popular children's literature
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ned moment that perfectly captures his religious extremism applied to pop culture

jokes.text: If the doctor asks why you cut it off, you caught me in bed with four beautiful women.
jokes.setup: Planning what to tell the doctor
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd attempt to make the injury sound impressive
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vanity trying to turn trauma into bragging rights

jokes.text: My Ferrari! I had to do awful things to pay for her.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's implication of corruption to afford luxury car
jokes.explanation: Dark humor suggesting police corruption delivered with Wiggum's characteristic cluelessness

jokes.text: But I have finger insurance! / A thumb is not a finger.
jokes.setup: Homer thinking he has relevant insurance
jokes.punchline: Technical medical/insurance loophole
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff about insurance bureaucracy and anatomical technicalities

jokes.text: Well, I could cut off the other thumb for a sense of symmetry.
jokes.setup: Doctor unable to help with reattachment
jokes.punchline: His casually offered alternative solution
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny medical 'solution' delivered with Hibbert's characteristic cheer

jokes.text: Hibbert's really losing it. We're going to Dr. Nick's.
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert suggesting cutting off other thumb
jokes.punchline: Marge's conclusion that they need the LESS qualified doctor
jokes.explanation: Great irony that the incompetent doctor seems more sensible than the qualified one

jokes.text: Just stick the old eye gouger in the pickle brine. That'll keep your thumb fresh and delicious.
jokes.setup: Asking Moe for ice for severed thumb
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate food preservation advice
jokes.explanation: Perfectly gross Moe response treating human body parts like bar snacks

jokes.text: Well, I really shouldn't, what with my massive blood loss and all. Although I do like the occasional beer.
jokes.punchline: Homer's medical awareness immediately undermined by his alcoholism
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-contradiction between health awareness and addiction

jokes.text: Did you ever see that Blue Man Group? Total rip-off of the Smurfs. And the Smurfs- They suck!
jokes.punchline: His bizarre comparison and escalating negativity
jokes.explanation: Absurd cultural analysis that becomes a running gag - Homer's confident wrongness

jokes.text: You and me share a common infirmity. If anyone ever tells you a hog won't eat a finger, they's lyin'.
jokes.punchline: His disturbing personal experience with pigs and digits
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny rural wisdom delivered with matter-of-fact authority

jokes.text: Inflammable means flammable? What a country!
jokes.punchline: His linguistic confusion and patriotic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay joke about English language confusion with perfect comedic timing and character voice

jokes.text: Somebody done 'stolded' my wheels!
jokes.punchline: His creative grammar in describing the theft
jokes.explanation: Solid character-based grammar humor that fits the rural stereotype

jokes.text: Say good-bye to your brother.
jokes.setup: Homer's thumb dying from lack of circulation
jokes.punchline: His dramatic farewell to his own body part
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic treatment of his thumb as family member is absurdly touching

jokes.text: Linguo... is... dead. [Powering Off]
jokes.punchline: Even in death, Linguo maintains proper grammar
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - the grammar robot corrects itself even while dying, brilliant commitment to the bit

jokes.text: Lie still. / I knew that. Just testing. / Sentence fragment. / 'Sentence fragment' is also a sentence fragment.
jokes.punchline: Robot recognizing its own grammatical hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where the grammar robot becomes self-aware of its own grammatical violations - sophisticated comedy writing

jokes.text: Must conserve battery power. [Powers Off]
jokes.setup: Robot has been correcting grammar extensively
jokes.punchline: Robot acknowledges its limitations and shuts down
jokes.explanation: Good robot logic - acknowledges its own power limitations

jokes.text: Idiot! You almost ran over a viewer! And she's in our key demo!
jokes.setup: Krusty nearly hitting someone with his car
jokes.punchline: His concern is purely about television demographics
jokes.explanation: Great Krusty moment showing his cynical entertainment industry mindset even in crisis

jokes.text: Huh? This isn't Miss Hoover's class. / I do not know this Mademoiselle Hoover of which you speak.
jokes.punchline: French teacher's overly formal response about not knowing Miss Hoover
jokes.explanation: Decent culture clash humor - French formality vs American casualness

jokes.text: What's happening? Where am I? / Sacre bleu! What a foolish question! You are at West Springfield Elementary School. / West Springfield? I'm at the wrong school!
jokes.punchline: Teacher calls her question foolish then reveals why she's confused
jokes.explanation: Setup for Lisa's wrong-school storyline - functional but not particularly funny

jokes.text: I guess they didn't have enough money to hire I.M. Pei. / Oh. You know about I.M. Pei?
jokes.punchline: Boy's surprise that someone else knows about high culture
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa finding an intellectual equal

jokes.text: My name's Thelonious. / As in Monk? / Yes. The esoteric appeal is worth the beatings.
jokes.setup: Boy introducing himself with unusual name
jokes.punchline: His acceptance of physical punishment for cultural sophistication
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy mixing intellectual pretension with childhood suffering

jokes.text: No can do, doll face. I got an informant wearing a wire. Just like on Nash Bridges.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum references outdated TV show for police tactics
jokes.explanation: Standard Wiggum incompetence - learning police work from TV

jokes.text: Why, I'd be delighted to sell you some illegally smuggled goods.
jokes.setup: Fat Tony being approached by undercover children
jokes.punchline: His immediate and explicit confession to crime
jokes.explanation: Fat Tony's complete lack of subtlety in criminal activity

jokes.text: My bad. I can't work my answering machine either.
jokes.setup: Accidentally revealing police operation over radio
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of technological incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum incompetence joke connecting police work failures to everyday tech struggles

jokes.text: Abracathumbra!
jokes.punchline: Magic word pun combining 'abracadabra' with 'thumb'
jokes.explanation: Clever pun that works with the character's missing digit situation

jokes.text: Dang! You could be one of them TV magic queers.
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate but earnest compliment about magicians
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent but relies on offensive language for humor - dated and problematic

jokes.text: This is where I come to cry.
jokes.setup: Milhouse shows Bart his discovery location
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of having a designated crying spot
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse characterization - pathetic but matter-of-fact about his misery

jokes.text: Bottle rockets, frog launchers, Weeping Mamas. Tijuana Toilet Crackers!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and specific firework names
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of made-up firework names with increasing absurdity

jokes.text: Don't worry. It's inflammable.
jokes.punchline: Using the confusing word that means the opposite of what it sounds like
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that works on multiple levels - both funny and ironic given the earlier explanation

jokes.text: Okay, drop the knife, Stabitha!
jokes.punchline: His made-up criminal nickname combining 'stab' with female name
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum wordplay showing his dramatic flair for police work

jokes.text: I can't go to juvie. They use guys like me as currency!
jokes.punchline: His specific fear about juvenile detention economics
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny prison economics joke delivered with Bart's characteristic street smarts

jokes.text: Yeah. They'll pass you around like- Well, like currency, like you said.
jokes.setup: Lou agreeing with Bart's currency analogy
jokes.punchline: His awkward self-censorship and circular logic
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up joke showing Lou's attempt to be supportive while staying appropriate

jokes.text: 'Hootie and the Blowfish'? / Yeah. It's cheaper than blank tape.
jokes.punchline: Police using outdated pop music tape for surveillance
jokes.explanation: Good police cheapness joke - using unpopular music tape instead of proper equipment

jokes.text: 'Hootie and the Blowfish'? / Yeah. It's cheaper than blank tape.
jokes.setup: Police using music tape for recording
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's budget-conscious explanation
jokes.explanation: Good joke about police department budget cuts and dated music references

jokes.text: Bad grammar overload! Error! Error! [Explosion]
jokes.setup: Robot exposed to multiple grammar violations
jokes.punchline: Literally exploding from linguistic overload
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the grammar robot concept - logical extreme of perfectionist character trait

jokes.text: He once pulled a slug out of my arm and inserted it into a stoolie's brain.
jokes.punchline: Casual description of reverse surgery/murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Fat Tony line delivered with matter-of-fact mob casualness

jokes.text: Boy, this sure was one crazy day.
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of the day's events
jokes.explanation: Standard sitcom ending understatement - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Boy, this sure was one crazy day.
jokes.setup: After entire episode of chaotic events
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of the day's events
jokes.explanation: Standard episode wrap-up understatement - functional but not particularly clever
